pr.title: Stop mixing game structs with sysdolphin
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Moved a block of item/game-specific struct definitions and item globals out of `include/sysdolphin/baselib/objalloc.h` and into `src/melee/it/item.h`. The PR rationale was header ownership: these definitions were not actually SysDolphin ObjAlloc structs and therefore did not belong in the baselib objalloc header.
what_changed: Removed 169 lines of game/item-specific typedefs and externs from `include/sysdolphin/baselib/objalloc.h`.
Added the same 169 lines to `src/melee/it/item.h`, near item helpers such as `GetItemDirect` and before item function declarations.
Relocated `ItemCommonData`, `Item_r13_Data`, `lbl_804D6D00`, `lbl_804D6D08`, `lbl_804D6D0C`, `lbl_804D6D10`, `lbl_804D6D14`, and `lbl_804D6D28` to the item header.

Also relocated several still-unknown allocation-adjacent structs currently named `HSD_ObjAllocUnk2`, `HSD_ObjAllocUnk`, `HSD_ObjAllocUnkStruct`, `HSD_ObjAllocUnk3`, `HSD_ObjAllocUnk4`, `HSD_ObjAllocUnk5`, and `HSD_ObjAllocUnk6` into `item.h`; despite their names, the PR states they were not actually ObjAlloc structs.
smart_moves: Separated engine/library headers from game-domain declarations instead of letting `sysdolphin` become a dumping ground for unrelated structs.
Preserved the struct layouts exactly while changing only ownership/location, minimizing matching risk and making the cleanup mechanically safe.
Kept uncertain names such as `HSD_ObjAllocUnk*` rather than inventing more specific names without evidence, while still moving them to the domain where they were actually used.
Moved `ItemCommonData` together with its associated item globals and `Item_r13_Data`, keeping related item data declarations colocated.
decomp_lessons: Do not place game-specific structs in SysDolphin/baselib headers just because they contain or sit near `HSD_ObjAllocData`; header ownership should follow the domain represented by the data, not merely a referenced type.
Large unknown structs can be relocated for architectural correctness without renaming or retyping fields, preserving decompilation stability while improving include hygiene.
When a symbol is known to be item-domain data, `src/melee/it/item.h` is a better temporary home than a low-level library header, even if some type names are still provisional.
Header cleanups are useful decomp work even when they do not improve matching directly, because they reduce cross-system coupling and clarify where future struct research should happen.
naming_conventions: Existing offset-style field names were retained, e.g. `x0`, `x4`, `x148`, `x14C`, and `x0_filler`.
Fields suspected or known to be floats used suffixes such as `x38_float`, `x6C_float`, and `x80_float`.
Unknown temporary type names used `Unk`/numbered naming, e.g. `HSD_ObjAllocUnk2`, `HSD_ObjAllocUnkStruct`, `HSD_ObjAllocUnk6`; the PR did not provide evidence for better names.
Item-domain r13 data used `Item_r13_Data` with descriptive members `item_common`, `common_items`, `adventure_items`, and `pokeball_items`.
Global symbols without recovered names remained label-based, e.g. `lbl_804D6D00` and `lbl_804D6D28`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This was a header relocation/type ownership cleanup, not an assembly matching PR; no new matching trick or codegen tactic is evident from the provided context.
The low-risk tactic was to move typedefs and extern declarations without changing field order, sizes, or primitive types.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were included in the slice.
The only explicit rationale from the PR body was that many item structs were mixed into `sysdolphin`'s `objalloc.h`, and they did not belong there because they were not actually ObjAlloc structs.
searchable_terms: Stop mixing game structs with sysdolphin
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further move `HSD_ObjAllocUnk*` types after they were placed in `src/melee/it/item.h`.
Search for uses of `ItemCommonData` and `Item_r13_Data` to determine whether they later gained more accurate field names.
Check whether any files still include `include/sysdolphin/baselib/objalloc.h` only to access item-domain types after this cleanup.
Investigate whether `HSD_ObjAllocUnkStruct` is actually item-owned despite retaining an `HSD_ObjAlloc`-style provisional name.
